items.scenario: You define history = [] in the class body of a Conversation class. Two conversations end up sharing the same messages.
items.question: Why, and how do you fix it?
items.answer: history is a class attribute shared by all instances, so appending through one instance mutates the single shared list. Move it into __init__ as self.history = [] so each instance gets its own list.

items.scenario: Your debugging output shows objects as <Message object at 0x10f...>, which is unhelpful.
items.question: What should you add?
items.answer: Implement __repr__ (and optionally __str__) to return a readable representation, e.g. f"Message(role={self.role!r}, content={self.content!r})". __repr__ is what the interpreter and logs use by default.

items.scenario: You want different tool classes (SearchTool, WeatherTool) to be usable interchangeably by an agent without a shared base class.
items.question: How does Python let them interoperate?
items.answer: Through duck typing: give each class the same method signature (e.g. run(query)). The agent calls tool.run(...) without caring about the class. Optionally formalize the contract with an abstract base class or typing.Protocol.
